question_text: A student places a plane mirror on a table and shines a light ray at an angle of 35° to the normal. According to the law of reflection, at what angle to the normal will the reflected ray leave the mirror?
explanation: The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection, both measured from the normal (an imaginary line perpendicular to the mirror surface). Since the light hits at 35° to the normal, it bounces off at 35° to the normal as well.
